mnemonics.title: OS Functions Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: DIPS: Device management, Input/Output, Process control, Storage management - FOUR MAIN FUNCTIONS TO REMEMBER

mnemonics.title: Process States
mnemonics.content: NEW READY RUNNING WAIT TERMINAL - N-R-R-W-T SEVEN STATES IN ORDER

mnemonics.title: Types of OS
mnemonics.content: BATCH MULTI TIME SHARED NETWORK DISTRIBUTED REAL - B-M-T-N-D-R RECOGNIZE SEVEN TYPES

mnemonics.title: CPU Scheduling Levels
mnemonics.content: LONG MEDIUM SHORT - LMS HIERARCHY FROM JOB ADMISSION TO CPU DISPATCH

acronyms.usage: The fundamental software managing computer resources
Visual interface with icons, windows, menus
Set of functions for application-OS interaction
CPU scheduling based on burst time
Time-sharing CPU scheduling with time quantum
Shared library loaded at runtime by OS

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a castle with inner fortress (kernel) and outer walls (user space). Only trusted guards (system calls) can enter the fortress.
Picture three people sharing one telephone - while one talks (CPU busy), others prepare their messages (I/O wait). All make efficient use of the phone.
Think of a library's online catalog - it represents more books than physically present, extending capacity through digital storage.
A factory assembly line: NEW (raw material), READY (waiting in queue), RUNNING (being processed), WAITING (repair break), TERMINATED (finished product).

analogies.analogy: Just as a government manages resources (roads, hospitals, schools) for citizens without them knowing infrastructure details, the OS manages hardware for applications.
The hotel manager allocates rooms (memory), handles guest requests (I/O), coordinates staff (processes), and ensures security - all invisible to guests.
The kernel is like a city mayor who makes critical decisions, manages resources, coordinates departments - essential for city functioning but behind the scenes.
Customers (applications) cannot enter the kitchen (kernel) directly. They communicate through waiters (system calls) who deliver requests and return results.
